content:
Good day everyone, I am deep in Rust and C FFIs - so at the moment I had build a script with environmental variables that I use Cargo to compile and run......I encountered this error and warning, can anybody please assist with simplifying what causes it and what it means, how to avoid it again ..

)-[~/Refactor_to_Rust/advanced_ffi/build-script-test]
└─$ cargo build
warning: corrupt incremental compilation artifact found at /home/mlawumba/Refactor_to_Rust/advanced_ffi/build-script-test/target/debug/incremental/build_script_build-0677bqguxpyds/s-hedkzn5ydj-15nj124-working/dep-graph.bin. This file will automatically be ignored and deleted. If you see this message repeatedly or can provoke it without manually manipulating the compiler's artifacts, please file an issue. The incremental compilation system relies on hardlinks and filesystem locks behaving correctly, and may not deal well with OS crashes, so whatever information you can provide about your filesystem or other state may be very relevant.
Have you tried to paste that message into a LLM (e.g. ChatGPT) and ask for analysis? Is the LLM answer you get helpful?

@Dirk Behme yeah I have - the LLM is suggesting something different from the Software book I am following, so I wanted to rather hear from a real person perhaps with Kernel experience than an LLM - since the panic is an Op system one..that's my reason to put it here..if that makes sense. thanks. I am trying to understand C / Rust bindgens as it is....so that's my dilemma right now. its fun though.

Like with #General > GNU Debugger @ 💬, this seems again a message intended for other forums.

This Zulip is about Rust for Linux, i.e. Rust code in the Linux kernel, not about userspace Rust code developed targeting Linux systems.

Please see Gary's message there:

Gary Guo said:

This sounds like a general question for Rust? These sort of questions are probably better asked at the Rust Users Forum or the Rust Language Discord.
You're welcome, and no problem! The name of the project can indeed lead to think it is about something else, so it is understandable :)
Yes and thanks Miguel, I love the project, I so wanna learn from it - listening to Rust/C/ Kernel people made me think this is the place - but I get it now, thanks.
